method_atom: An exact duality transformation that maps the dynamics of a nonlinear system onto that of its canonically conjugate variable, so that the equation of motion for one variable is identical in form to the equation for its conjugate. The governing dynamics is that of a damped, driven, inertial (second-order) nonlinear oscillator analogous to a periodically forced pendulum, in which an inertia term is essential for the mapping to be exact; under an external periodic drive the oscillator phase-locks, producing quantized plateaus (locking steps) in its driven response, obtained via quasi-classical analysis and numerical computation of the response characteristics over a range of parameters.
object_atom: Superconducting circuits made of small Josephson junctions — a single junction embedded in an inductive environment and a long chain of junctions supporting quantum phase slips — whose conjugate quasi-charge dynamics under microwave irradiation yields current-Shapiro steps in the current-voltage characteristic, of interest as a metrological current standard.
brief_reason: The conjugate-variable duality plus the driven inertial-oscillator phase-locking machinery is transferable across fields, whereas the Josephson-junction circuits, quasi-charge/quantum-phase-slip variables, and metrological current standard are the physics-specific object.
